title: CompletionVerdict
description: Output of the rebar.llm completion-verification operation (`rebar verify-completion <id>`): a PASS/FAIL verdict that a ticket's completion criteria (acceptance/success/close/completion criteria, definitions of done; for bugs, resolution) are demonstrably met by the implementation. SHAPE-ONLY: the FAIL⇔findings invariant and verdict normalization are enforced by the operation's `_reconcile` (code), not the schema, because the runner validates this schema BEFORE the operation can repair a sloppy model turn. `verdict` is `string` (not a strict enum) so the operation can normalize casing. Each FAIL finding reuses the canonical `finding` shape with an added `criterion` (the specific requirement that failed). The MCP `verify_completion` tool returns this shape as a plain dict but advertises no outputSchema (live LLM call — a documented NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT).

verdict.description: PASS or FAIL (normalized by the operation; not a strict enum so model casing can be repaired).

findings.description: One finding per FAILING criterion (empty on PASS). Reuses the canonical `finding` shape; the failing requirement rides on `finding.criterion` (an additional property).

summary.description: Optional verifier summary; carries the no-explicit-criteria PASS rationale when applicable.

model.description: Model id used (null for non-LLM/fake runners).

trace_id.description: Langfuse trace id when tracing is enabled; null otherwise.
